summary: Function-level final coverage review completed for 3 main functions. Average final coverage score: 100.

function_reviews.summary: All 31 provided scenarios for the User Registration function are fully covered by 41 test cases. Test cases are traceable to requirement IDs, clear, executable, and verifiable. Coverage includes positive, negative, validation, boundary, business rule, security, UX/UI, and integration categories as per the test scope. Previous coverage issues have been resolved. No missing scenarios or weak test cases identified. The test cases are ready for QA execution.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review noted that integration scenarios for email verification system and token expiration/resend logic were not explicitly covered. Additional test cases (TC032-TC041) have been added to cover these integration and business rule scenarios.
resolved_issues.resolution: Added 10 integration and business rule test cases covering email verification system dependency, token expiration (24 hours), resend cooldown (60 seconds), boundary timing for resend, and rate limiting on resend.

final_recommendations.description: TC006 (Register with password length exactly 12 characters) uses test data 'Abc123@#xyz' which is 12 characters and meets complexity rules. However, the test steps are generic and do not specify the exact values. Update test steps to include the exact password value for clarity and repeatability.

TC021 (Register with password containing single spaces) uses test data 'Pass1@ word' which contains a single space. The expected results state that registration should succeed. However, the requirement does not explicitly state that single spaces are allowed; only consecutive spaces are forbidden. Confirm with the product owner that single spaces are permitted in passwords. If not, update the test case to be negative.

TC033 and TC040 both test the 24-hour expiration boundary but use different expected results for the same time offset (24 hours and 1 minute). TC033 expects an error message, TC040 expects the same. This is consistent, but ensure the test environment can reliably simulate 24-hour time jumps. Consider using a mock or time manipulation tool to avoid long wait times.

function_reviews.summary: All 16 scenarios for the Email Verification function are covered by 29 test cases. Positive, negative, boundary, security, and integration scenarios are fully addressed. Test cases are traceable to requirement IDs and are clear, executable, and verifiable. No missing scenarios or weak test cases identified. Previous coverage issues have been resolved.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review had mismatched test case IDs (e.g., TC032 instead of TC042).
resolved_issues.resolution: Test case IDs have been updated to match the final test case list (TC042 to TC070).

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review did not include boundary test cases for link expiration (just before 24 hours, exactly at 24 hours, at 24 hours and 1 second).
resolved_issues.resolution: Added TC059 (valid just before 24 hours), TC060 (expired exactly at 24 hours), and TC067 (expired at 24 hours and 1 second) to cover boundary conditions for link expiration.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review did not include test cases for verification link format and token expiration storage.
resolved_issues.resolution: Added TC063 (verification link format) and TC066 (token expiration storage) to cover these aspects.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous coverage review did not include test cases for resend after expiration and login after multiple resends.
resolved_issues.resolution: Added TC061 (verification after resend), TC064 (expiration after resend), TC068 (expired link after resend), TC069 (login blocked after resend), and TC070 (verification after multiple resends) to cover these scenarios.

final_recommendations.description: TC044 (Verification link expires after 24 hours) and TC045 (Attempt login with expired verification link) are very similar. Consider merging them or clarifying the difference in preconditions to avoid redundancy.
TC065 (Login blocked after multiple failed verification attempts) is not directly traceable to a specific requirement. Consider adding a related requirement ID or removing if not needed.

function_reviews.summary: All provided scenarios for the main function are fully covered by the final test cases. The single scenario SC030 is covered by 14 test cases (TC071 to TC083 and TC048 from the previous review). All test cases are traceable to requirement IDs BR006 and FR004. Previous coverage issues have been resolved. Test cases are clear, executable, and verifiable. No missing scenarios or weak test cases were identified. Coverage aligns with the test scope for negative, positive, boundary, business rule, and integration categories.

resolved_issues.issue: Previous review only had one test case (TC048) for scenario SC030, missing coverage for resend timing, boundary conditions, and multiple resend scenarios.
resolved_issues.resolution: Added 13 additional test cases (TC071 to TC083) covering negative, positive, boundary, and multiple resend scenarios for account state after 24 hours without verification.
